name: Sherlock
one_liner: Open-source OSINT CLI tool that hunts down social media accounts by username across 400+ social networks.
access_notes: Fully open-source (MIT). Installable via pipx, Docker, Homebrew, and Linux package managers. Runs entirely locally as a CLI with no account, API key, or payment required. An official self-hosted REST API wrapper (sherlock-project/api) also exists with no auth.
api_notes: Primary interface is a CLI (`sherlock <username>`) with flags for output formats (TXT/CSV/XLSX), proxy, site filtering, NSFW inclusion, and browser opening. A separate official repo (sherlock-project/api) provides a self-hosted Django REST Framework wrapper exposing username-search endpoints, but it is minimally maintained and self-host only. Effectively a handful of operations centered on username lookup.
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped MCP server. Multiple community MCP implementations exist (e.g., Burnsedia/sherlock-mcp using FastMCP, p1atdev/sherlock-mcp, qKitNp/sherlock_mcp, and an Apify-hosted actor MCP endpoint), but none are published by the sherlock-project organization.
main_blocker: None for local use. It is a local CLI / self-hosted tool rather than a hosted SaaS API, so integration means wrapping the CLI or self-hosting the REST API; there is no vendor-hosted endpoint to call.

evidence.claim: Sherlock is a CLI OSINT tool that hunts social media accounts by username across 400+ networks, installed via pipx/Docker/package managers.
Official sherlock-project/api repo is a self-hosted Django REST Framework API with no documented authentication, run locally at 127.0.0.1:8000.
Community MCP server wraps Sherlock via FastMCP for username searches across 400+ platforms (no vendor MCP).

open_questions: The self-hosted REST API appears minimally maintained; unclear how well it tracks the current CLI version. No hosted/managed offering exists from the project.
